description_long: This object is a primitive, triangular stone blade, likely an ancient or improvised knife. The blade is made from a single piece of gray stone, chipped and flaked to form a sharp edge along one side and a pointed tip. The opposite side is thicker and blunter, serving as a rudimentary handle, with no clear separation between blade and grip. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible flake scars and a matte finish, indicating it was shaped by hand. There are no visible decorative elements or ergonomic features, and the object appears utilitarian. The overall form is simple, functional, and characteristic of early stone tools or arrowheads.
description: A roughly chipped, triangular gray stone blade with a sharp edge and pointed tip, lacking a distinct handle or ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full triangular profile, sharp edge, and pointed tip.
description_view_1: Edge-on view revealing the thinness and flaked cross-section of the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible surface markings and a slightly blunter base.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again highlighting the thin, chipped profile.
attribute_handle_design: integral with blade, blunt stone base, no ergonomic features
